title: Torrubia suborbiculata

This family consists of over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are flowering plants native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Torrubia is part of the Polygonaceae family and is primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Torrubia suborbiculata, including its order and rank, may require further research. uses: Torrubia suborbiculata, also known as the 'round-leaved torrubia', is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. It is also used to treat gastrointestinal problems like diarrhea and dysentery. Furthermore, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. The plant's medicinal properties are largely attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids.
